member.name: Jo Platt
member.synopsis: Jo Platt is the Labour (Co-op) MP for Leigh and Atherton , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Music Venues
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps her Department is taking to support grassroot music venues.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Grassroots music venues are vital to the UK’s music culture, offering emerging artists a platform, supporting local economies and jobs, and giving thousands the joy of live music up close. We are committed to supporting the sustainability of the entire li...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.answerText: It is normal for social security schemes to contain different provisions for people at different stages of their lives, which reflect varying priorities and circumstances. Personal Independence Payment (PIP) claimants continue to receive the benefit after...

writtenQuestions.heading: Music Venues: Tickets
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, if she will make an assessment with Cabinet colleagues of the potential merits of reducing VAT on cultural ticketing in Grassroots Music Venues.
writtenQuestions.answerText: VAT is a broad-based tax on consumption, and the 20 per cent standard rate applies to most goods and services. VAT is the UK’s third largest tax, forecast to raise £180 billion in 2025/26.Tax breaks reduce the revenue available for vital public services a...

writtenQuestions.heading: Retail Trade: Crime
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, how many local authorities have applied for Government funding to support Trading Standards to tackle illicit activity on high streets; and if he will provide a breakdown by loca
writtenQuestions.answerText: Tackling high street criminality is a priority for this government. On 19 May, the government announced a £30 million crackdown on illegal activity on the high street, over three years. £20 million of this will fund an enhanced law enforcement response an...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: ADHD Taskforce
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential implications for his Department's policies of the Report of the independent ADHD Taskforce.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England established an independent attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) taskforce which brought together those with lived experience with experts from the National Health Service, education, charity, and justice sectors to get a better unde...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Long Covid: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if we will make an assessment of the potential merits of implementing centrally-funded specialist hubs to support patients with Long Covid across the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since April 2024, in line with the National Health Service operating framework and the establishment of integrated care systems, the commissioning of services to support patients with Long Covid has been the responsibility of integrated care boards. Where...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Children: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what actions he is taking to ensure every ICB is required to provide specialist post-infection services for children in line with NICE guidance.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Services for post-infection conditions are not classified as specialised under NHS England specialised commissioning and are the responsibility local of integrated care boards which are accountable for providing appropriate services in the local area.Pati...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: The government recognises that decisions about demolition and redevelopment or retrofit have implications for the built environment. The research in question is intended to support our understanding of existing practice and to inform areas of improvement ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: North West
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, if she will make an assessment of the potential impact of (a) Northern Powerhouse Rail and (b) the Liverpool–Manchester Railway on (i) economic growth, (ii) productivity and (iii) job creation in the North West
writtenQuestions.answerText: Northern Powerhouse Rail (NPR) will drive growth and productivity across the Northern Growth Corridor by delivering a turn up and go railway from Liverpool to York. Our Northern Growth Strategy recognises significant opportunity to accelerate economic gro...

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: North West
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the potential merits of (a) station upgrades and (b) improved integration between national rail services and devolved transport systems across the North-West rail netw
writtenQuestions.answerText: Greater integration between national rail and devolved transport systems like Greater Manchester’s Bee Network is key to improving connectivity within, and between, our great cities in the North. Our newly published Better Connected national transport str...

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: North of England
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps her Department is taking to (a) consult and (b) communicate with the North of England's (i) mayoral combined authorities, (ii) local authorities and (ii) local stakeholders and communities on the (A)
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Northern Growth Strategy commits Government to working in partnership with Mayoral Strategic Authorities (MSAs) and local leaders to deliver Northern Powerhouse Rail (NPR). This is set out in the Compact Agreements with Mayors. We have established new...

writtenQuestions.heading: Construction: Skilled Workers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department is taking to support skills development in retrofit and traditional construction methods, including heritage crafts such as stonemasonry.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 20 May, we published the Post-16 Pathways Implementation plan, setting out our plan for post-16 technical and vocational qualifications. It sets out that we are exploring options with the sector for introducing an Occupational Certificate in stonemason...

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs: Armed Forces
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment her Department has made of trends in the level of variation between local authorities in the provision of SEND support for children from Armed Forces families.

writtenQuestions.heading: Construction: VAT

writtenQuestions.heading: Carbon Emissions: Buildings
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether his Department is taking steps to support embodied carbon reduction through the reuse and retrofit of existing buildings.
writtenQuestions.answerText: National design planning practice guidance highlights that reusing and adapting buildings can reduce embodied carbon emissions and waste, and lower resource consumption while improving energy performance. Through the Warm Homes Plan, we are investing £15 ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Regeneration: Cultural Heritage
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment he has made of the role heritage-led regeneration can play in supporting the renewal of town centres and local economic growth.
writtenQuestions.answerText: National planning policy is clear about the positive contribution that the conservation of heritage assets can make to sustainable communities, including their economic viability. Between 16 December 2025 and 10 March 2026, we consulted on a new National ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
